literature.note: Prescribed textbooks vary by school. Common texts include:
prose: Short Stories Collection (as prescribed by CISCE)
drama: The Merchant of Venice (Shakespeare) — OR — As prescribed

1_Rational_Numbers.title: Fraction to Decimal — Denominator Check
1_Rational_Numbers.trick: If denominator has only 2s and 5s as prime factors, the decimal terminates. Example: 3/8 = 0.375 (8 = 2³). If denominator has prime factors other than 2 or 5, decimal repeats. Example: 1/3 = 0.333...

1_Rational_Numbers.title: Comparing Fractions — Cross Multiply
1_Rational_Numbers.trick: To compare a/b vs c/d, compare a×d vs b×c. Example: 3/7 vs 5/11 → 3×11=33 vs 7×5=35 → 3/7 < 5/11. No LCM needed.

1_Rational_Numbers.title: Adding Fractions — Bow Tie Method
1_Rational_Numbers.trick: a/b + c/d = (ad + bc)/bd. Then simplify. Example: 2/3 + 1/4 = (8+3)/12 = 11/12. Works for 2 fractions in one step.

2_Exponents_and_Powers.title: Any number to power 0 = 1
2_Exponents_and_Powers.trick: a⁰ = 1 for any a≠0. Reason: aⁿ/aⁿ = a⁰ = 1. Common exam trap: (-5)⁰ = 1, not -1 or 0.

2_Exponents_and_Powers.title: Negative Exponents = Reciprocal
2_Exponents_and_Powers.trick: a⁻ⁿ = 1/aⁿ. Example: 2⁻³ = 1/8. Flip the base and make exponent positive.

2_Exponents_and_Powers.title: Multiplying Same Base — Add Exponents
2_Exponents_and_Powers.trick: aᵐ × aⁿ = aᵐ⁺ⁿ. Never multiply the bases! Example: 2³ × 2⁴ = 2⁷ = 128, NOT 4⁷.

3_Squares_and_Square_Roots.title: Square of numbers ending in 5
3_Squares_and_Square_Roots.trick: For n5²: Write 25, then multiply n×(n+1) and prefix. Example: 85² = (8×9)=72 + 25 = 7225. Works for ANY number ending in 5: 105² = (10×11)=110 + 25 = 11025.

3_Squares_and_Square_Roots.title: Square Root by Prime Factorization Shortcut
3_Squares_and_Square_Roots.trick: Group prime factors in pairs. Each pair contributes one factor to the root. √3600 = √(2²×2²×3²×5²) = 2×2×3×5 = 60.

3_Squares_and_Square_Roots.title: Square Root Estimation — Guess & Check
3_Squares_and_Square_Roots.trick: For √N: Find nearest perfect square below N. √50: 7²=49, 7.1²=50.41 → √50≈7.07. Refine: (Guess + N/Guess)/2. (7+50/7)/2 = (7+7.14)/2 = 7.07.

4_Cubes_and_Cube_Roots.title: Cube of numbers ending in 1, 4, 5, 6, 9
4_Cubes_and_Cube_Roots.trick: The cube ends with the same digit: 1³=1, 4³=64, 5³=125, 6³=216, 9³=729. Useful for verification.

4_Cubes_and_Cube_Roots.title: Cube Root of Perfect Cubes (≤6 digits)
4_Cubes_and_Cube_Roots.trick: Memorize: 1³=1, 2³=8, 3³=27, 4³=64, 5³=125, 6³=216, 7³=343, 8³=512, 9³=729, 10³=1000. For N=17576: Last 3 digits=576→ends in 6→last digit 6. Remaining 17→between 2³(8) and 3³(27)→tens digit 2. Answer: 26. Check: 26³=17576 ✓

5_Playing_with_Numbers.title: Divisibility by 2, 4, 8
5_Playing_with_Numbers.trick: Last digit even → ÷2. Last 2 digits ÷4 → ÷4. Last 3 digits ÷8 → ÷8. Example: 7128: last 3 digits 128÷8=16 → ÷8.

5_Playing_with_Numbers.title: Divisibility by 3, 9
5_Playing_with_Numbers.trick: Sum of digits ÷3 → number ÷3. Sum ÷9 → number ÷9. Example: 5841→5+8+4+1=18→÷3 and ÷9.

5_Playing_with_Numbers.title: Divisibility by 7 — Shortcut
5_Playing_with_Numbers.trick: Double the last digit, subtract from remaining number. Repeat until small. Example: 343→34−6=28→÷7. 511→51−2=49→÷7.

5_Playing_with_Numbers.title: Multiply by 11 Instantly
5_Playing_with_Numbers.trick: For 2-digit: AB×11 = A_(A+B)_B. Example: 63×11 = 6_(6+3)_3 = 693. If A+B≥10, carry: 78×11 = 7_(15)_8 = 858. For 3-digit: ABC×11 = A_(A+B)_(B+C)_C with carries.

5_Playing_with_Numbers.title: Vedic: Multiply Numbers Near 50
5_Playing_with_Numbers.trick: For 50+a and 50+b: (25 + (a+b)/2) hundred + ab. Example: 53×57: a=3, b=7. (25+5)=30 hundred + 21 = 3021. Works when a+b is even.

5_Playing_with_Numbers.title: Multiply 2-Digit Numbers — Criss-Cross (Vedic)
5_Playing_with_Numbers.trick: AB × CD: Units = B×D. Tens = A×D + B×C. Hundreds = A×C. With carries. Example: 23×41: units=3×1=3, tens=2×1+3×4=14→carry 1, hundreds=2×4+1=9. Answer: 943.

7_Percent_and_Percentage.title: 10% — Just Move the Decimal
7_Percent_and_Percentage.trick: 10% of any number = shift decimal left once. 10% of 847 = 84.7. Then: 5% = half of 10%. 1% = shift twice. 20% = 2×10%.

7_Percent_and_Percentage.title: X% of Y = Y% of X
7_Percent_and_Percentage.trick: 4% of 75 = 75% of 4 = 3. Swap when it makes calculation easier. Example: 16% of 25 = 25% of 16 = 4.

7_Percent_and_Percentage.title: Successive Percentage Change
7_Percent_and_Percentage.trick: If price increases by a% then b%, effective change = a + b + (ab/100)%. Example: +20% then +10% = 20+10+2 = 32% net increase (not 30%). For discount then discount, use negatives.

8_Profit_and_Loss.title: CP from SP — Direct Formula
8_Profit_and_Loss.trick: If gain% = g: CP = SP × 100/(100+g). If loss% = l: CP = SP × 100/(100-l). No need to set up equations.

8_Profit_and_Loss.title: Marked Price vs Discount
8_Profit_and_Loss.trick: MP is always the base. Discount% is on MP, not CP. Selling Price = MP × (100-D%)/100. Common mistake: applying discount% to CP.

9_Simple_and_Compound_Interest.title: CI Shortcut — 2 Years
9_Simple_and_Compound_Interest.trick: CI for 2 years = P[(1+r/100)²-1] = P(2r/100 + r²/10000). Difference between CI and SI for 2 years = P(r/100)².

9_Simple_and_Compound_Interest.title: CI Shortcut — 3 Years
9_Simple_and_Compound_Interest.trick: CI for 3 years = P[3r/100 + 3r²/10000 + r³/1000000]. Difference CI-SI for 3 years = P(r/100)² × (3+r/100).

9_Simple_and_Compound_Interest.title: Doubling Time — Rule of 72
9_Simple_and_Compound_Interest.trick: Money doubles in approximately 72/r years at r% compound interest. Example: At 8% pa, money doubles in 72/8 = 9 years. Useful for quick estimation.

14_Linear_Equations.title: Cross Multiplication — Direct
14_Linear_Equations.trick: For a/b = c/d, cross multiply: a×d = b×c. Example: (x+2)/3 = 5/4 → 4(x+2) = 15 → 4x+8 = 15 → x = 7/4.

14_Linear_Equations.title: Checking Your Answer
14_Linear_Equations.trick: Plug answer back into original equation. LHS should equal RHS. If x=7/4: (7/4+2)/3 = (15/4)/3 = 5/4 = 1.25. RHS = 5/4 = 1.25 ✓.

22_Surface_Area_Volume.title: Cylinder — Curved vs Total Surface
22_Surface_Area_Volume.trick: CSA = 2πrh (label around can). TSA = 2πrh + 2πr² (label + top & bottom). Memory: CSA = 'Circumference × height'.

22_Surface_Area_Volume.title: Cone — Slant Height
22_Surface_Area_Volume.trick: l = √(r²+h²). Then CSA = πrl, TSA = πr(l+r). Always find slant height first. Common mistake: using h instead of l in CSA.

physics.title: Density Trick
physics.trick: Density = Mass/Volume. Unit: g/cm³ or kg/m³. Water density = 1 g/cm³ = 1000 kg/m³. Objects denser than water sink. Less dense float.

physics.title: Pressure — Smaller Area = More Pressure
physics.trick: P = F/A. Same force on smaller area = higher pressure. Explains why nails have sharp points and snowshoes are wide.

chemistry.title: Atomic Number vs Mass Number
chemistry.trick: Atomic Number (Z) = number of protons = number of electrons (neutral atom). Mass Number (A) = protons + neutrons. Neutrons = A - Z.

chemistry.title: Balancing Equations — Save H and O for Last
chemistry.trick: Balance metals first, then non-metals, then hydrogen, then oxygen last. Count atoms on each side after every step.

Algebra_Expansions.title: (a+b)² ≠ a² + b² — Common Mistake
Algebra_Expansions.trick: (a+b)² = a² + 2ab + b². The middle term 2ab is the most missed. Example: (x+3)² = x² + 6x + 9, NOT x² + 9.

Algebra_Expansions.title: (a+b)(a-b) = a² - b² — Direct
Algebra_Expansions.trick: When you see conjugate pairs, go straight to difference of squares. Example: (2x+5)(2x-5) = 4x² - 25. No FOIL needed.

Algebra_Expansions.title: Expansion Pattern Recognition
Algebra_Expansions.trick: (a+b+c)² = a²+b²+c²+2ab+2bc+2ca. Memorize: square each + double each pair. Example: (x+y+3)² = x²+y²+9+2xy+6y+6x.

Algebra_Factorisation.title: Factor by Grouping — Split Middle Term
Algebra_Factorisation.trick: For ax²+bx+c: Find two numbers that multiply to a×c and add to b. Example: 6x²+13x+5 → numbers multiplying to 30 and adding to 13 = 10, 3 → 6x²+10x+3x+5 = 2x(3x+5)+1(3x+5) = (2x+1)(3x+5).

Algebra_Factorisation.title: Recognizing Perfect Squares
Algebra_Factorisation.trick: a²+2ab+b² = (a+b)². Check: first and last terms must be perfect squares, middle = 2×√first×√last. Example: 4x²+12x+9 = (2x+3)² because √4x²=2x, √9=3, 2×2x×3=12x ✓.

Algebra_Factorisation.title: Difference of Squares — Always Check First
Algebra_Factorisation.trick: a²−b² = (a+b)(a−b). Before trying complex factorisation, check if expression is just difference of two squares. Example: x⁴−16 = (x²+4)(x²−4) = (x²+4)(x+2)(x−2).

Algebra_Indices_Logarithms.title: Log Base Change — The Bridge Formula
Algebra_Indices_Logarithms.trick: log_a(b) = log_c(b)/log_c(a). Pick c = 10 for calculator work. Example: log_2(32) = log_10(32)/log_10(2) = 1.5051/0.3010 = 5. Or recognize 2⁵=32 directly.

Algebra_Indices_Logarithms.title: Log of 1 and Log of Base
Algebra_Indices_Logarithms.trick: log_a(1) = 0 (any base). log_a(a) = 1. These two show up in nearly every log simplification problem.

Algebra_Indices_Logarithms.title: Log Product/Quotient Rules
Algebra_Indices_Logarithms.trick: log(MN) = log M + log N. log(M/N) = log M - log N. log(Mⁿ) = n log M. Always simplify using these before calculating.

Geometry_Triangles.title: Angle Sum — Check Your Work
Geometry_Triangles.trick: In any triangle, A+B+C = 180°. If you get anything else, redo. In right triangle, other two add to 90°.

Geometry_Triangles.title: Congruence Rules — S A S, A S A, S S S, R H S
Geometry_Triangles.trick: SAS (not ASS!), ASA, SSS, RHS (right angle-hypotenuse-side). Memory aid: 'SAS is not ASS because ASS is not a theorem.'

Geometry_Triangles.title: Pythagoras — Check if Triple
Geometry_Triangles.trick: Common triples: (3,4,5), (5,12,13), (7,24,25), (8,15,17), (9,40,41). If you spot these, hypotenuse is instant. Multiples also work: (6,8,10), (15,36,39).

Trigonometry.title: SOH CAH TOA — Permanent Memory
Trigonometry.trick: Sin = Opposite/Hypotenuse. Cos = Adjacent/Hypotenuse. Tan = Opposite/Adjacent = Sin/Cos.

Trigonometry.title: Standard Angles — Finger Method
Trigonometry.trick: For sin: 0°=0, 30°=1/2, 45°=1/√2, 60°=√3/2, 90°=1. For cos: reverse the order. For tan: sin/cos.

Trigonometry.title: Complementary Angles
Trigonometry.trick: sin(90°-θ) = cos θ. cos(90°-θ) = sin θ. tan(90°-θ) = cot θ. Use to simplify expressions: sin 30° × cos 60° = (1/2)(1/2) = 1/4.

Commercial_GST.title: GST = Output Tax - Input Tax
Commercial_GST.trick: Output GST = tax collected from customer. Input GST = tax paid to supplier. Net GST payable = Output - Input. If input > output, you get refund.

Commercial_GST.title: GST Rate Application
Commercial_GST.trick: GST is always calculated on selling price, then added. Total bill = SP + GST% of SP = SP × (1 + GST%/100). Never calculate backwards from total without formula.

Algebra_Quadratic_Equations.title: Discriminant — Nature of Roots in 1 Second
Algebra_Quadratic_Equations.trick: For ax²+bx+c=0, D = b²-4ac. D > 0 → real & distinct. D = 0 → real & equal. D < 0 → no real roots (imaginary). Don't solve — just check D.

Algebra_Quadratic_Equations.title: Sum and Product of Roots — Direct
Algebra_Quadratic_Equations.trick: If α, β are roots of ax²+bx+c=0: α+β = -b/a, αβ = c/a. Example: 2x²-6x+4=0 → α+β=3, αβ=2. No need to find roots.

Algebra_Quadratic_Equations.title: Quadratic Formula — The Singer's Method
Algebra_Quadratic_Equations.trick: x = [-b ± √(b²-4ac)] / 2a. Sing it: 'x equals minus b, plus or minus, square root of b squared minus four a c, ALL over two a.' Never forget it.

Algebra_Quadratic_Equations.title: Factor by Splitting — Quick Check
Algebra_Quadratic_Equations.trick: Before using quadratic formula, try: find two numbers multiplying to ac and adding to b. If integers exist within 10 seconds, factorize. If not, use formula.

Algebra_Ratio_Proportion.title: K-Method — Universal Proportion Solver
Algebra_Ratio_Proportion.trick: If a/b = c/d = e/f = k, then a=bk, c=dk, e=fk. Replace all variables with k-expressions. Example: If a:b = 2:3 and b:c = 4:5, find a:b:c. a:b=2:3=8:12, b:c=4:5=12:15 → a:b:c=8:12:15.

Algebra_Ratio_Proportion.title: Fourth, Third, Mean Proportional
Algebra_Ratio_Proportion.trick: Fourth proportional of a,b,c: a:b = c:x → x = bc/a. Third proportional of a,b: a:b = b:x → x = b²/a. Mean proportional of a,b: a:x = x:b → x = √(ab).

Mensuration.title: Sphere — Don't Confuse with Hemisphere
Mensuration.trick: Sphere: V=4/3πr³, SA=4πr². Hemisphere: V=2/3πr³, CSA=2πr², TSA=3πr². Hemisphere TSA has 3 faces (curved + flat base).

Mensuration.title: Frustum of Cone — Volume Direct
Mensuration.trick: V = πh/3 × (R² + r² + Rr). Where R = bottom radius, r = top radius. Derivation not needed in exam — use formula directly.

Mensuration.title: Combine Shapes — Sum of Volumes
Mensuration.trick: Cylinder + Cone on top: V = πr²h₁ + (1/3)πr²h₂ = πr²(h₁ + h₂/3). Only works if base radii match.

Trigonometry.title: Height & Distance — Angle of Elevation/Depression
Trigonometry.trick: Draw diagram. Label: observer = ground level. Elevation = looking UP from horizontal. Depression = looking DOWN from horizontal. Both measured from horizontal line.

Trigonometry.title: Which Ratio to Use
Trigonometry.trick: If you have: Opposite & Hypotenuse → sin. Adjacent & Hypotenuse → cos. Opposite & Adjacent → tan. Connected to unknown AND known is what you need.

Trigonometry.title: tan θ = sin θ / cos θ — The Universal Simplifier
Trigonometry.trick: When simplifying trig expressions, convert everything to sin and cos first. Example: (sin θ + tan θ)/(1 + sec θ) = sin θ after simplification.

Statistics.title: Mean — Assumed Mean Method
Statistics.trick: For large data: Pick a convenient assumed mean A. Find d = x-A for each. Mean = A + Σfd/Σf. Much faster than adding all values.

Statistics.title: Median — Cumulative Frequency Quick
Statistics.trick: Median class = where cumulative frequency first exceeds N/2. Then: Median = L + [(N/2 - cf)/f] × h. L = lower limit of median class, cf = cum freq before median class.

Statistics.title: Mode — Z = 3M - 2X̄ (Empirical)
Statistics.trick: For moderately skewed data: Mode ≈ 3×Median - 2×Mean. Quick check when exact mode calculation is complex.

Probability.title: P(Event) = Favourable/Total
Probability.trick: Count favorable outcomes first, total outcomes second. P = F/T. Always 0 ≤ P ≤ 1. P=0 → impossible. P=1 → certain.

Probability.title: P(not E) = 1 - P(E)
Probability.trick: When counting 'not' is easier, use complement. Example: P(at least one head in 2 coin tosses) = 1 - P(no heads) = 1 - 1/4 = 3/4.

Light.title: Lens Formula Sign Convention
Light.trick: For convex lens: f is +, u is -, v is + (real image) or - (virtual). 1/f = 1/v - 1/u. Use proper signs or answer will be wrong.

Light.title: Magnification Formula
Light.trick: m = v/u = hi/ho = (f)/(f+u). Positive m = erect & virtual. Negative m = inverted & real. |m|>1 = enlarged. |m|<1 = diminished.

Light.title: Mirror vs Lens — Don't Confuse
Light.trick: Mirror: 1/f = 1/v + 1/u. Lens: 1/f = 1/v - 1/u. Mirror formula has PLUS, lens has MINUS between 1/v and 1/u.

Electricity.title: Ohm's Law — The Triangle
Electricity.trick: V = IR. Cover what you want: Cover V → I×R. Cover I → V÷R. Cover R → V÷I. Unit check: V(volts), I(amps), R(ohms).

Electricity.title: Power — Three Forms
Electricity.trick: P = VI = I²R = V²/R. Use based on what's given: V and I → VI. I and R → I²R. V and R → V²/R.

Electricity.title: Series vs Parallel
Electricity.trick: Series: R_eq = R₁+R₂+R₃..., current same, voltage divides. Parallel: 1/R_eq = 1/R₁+1/R₂+..., voltage same, current divides. Memory: 'Series adds, Parallel divides.'

Mole_Concept.title: Mole = Given Mass / Molar Mass
Mole_Concept.trick: Number of moles = mass(g) / molar mass(g/mol). Then: molecules = moles × 6.022×10²³. Volume at STP = moles × 22.4 L.

Mole_Concept.title: Empirical vs Molecular Formula
Mole_Concept.trick: Empirical = simplest ratio. Molecular = actual. n = molecular mass / empirical mass. Example: CH₂O (empirical), C₆H₁₂O₆ (molecular, n=6).
